channel.title: Supernatural on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Supernatural on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Abu Fanous: The Mysterious Light Phenomenon in the Arabian Desert
item.description:
Abu Fanous is a mysterious light phenomenon observed in the Arabian desert, often appearing at dawn or night as a moving orb that lures travelers into remote areas before vanishing. In Arabic folklore, it is described as a jinn that causes people to become lost and die, with advice to recite religious verses to ward it off. While theories suggest natural gases igniting, no clear scientific explanation exists for Abu Fanous. The phenomenon has inspired art and video games, and there are accounts of travelers encountering it.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abu_Fanous

item.title: New Lands
item.description:
New Lands is the second nonfiction book by Charles Fort, published in 1923 by Boni & Liveright. The book primarily addresses astronomical anomalies and is described as a satirical critique of astronomers. Fort criticizes astronomical predictions and scientific dogma, expanding on his theories of a “Super-Sargasso Sea” and continents above Earth. Despite its content, New Lands is considered Fort’s worst-regarded book, with its speculations on sky continents and Earth’s shape being seen as dated. The book had poor sales, leading to Boni & Liveright declining to publish Fort’s subsequent work.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Lands
